subject: Physics
title: The Event Horizon Telescope Image
description: A fast breakdown of The Event Horizon Telescope Image for builders.

hook.text: In 2019, eight radio telescopes across the globe combined to photograph a black hole for the first time.

buildup.text: The target: M87*, a supermassive black hole 55 million light-years away, 6.5 billion times the Sun's mass.

discovery.text: The image showed a bright ring of superheated gas surrounding a dark shadow—the black hole's event horizon.

twist.text: The data was so massive it had to be shipped on hard drives by airplane. Internet transfer would have taken too long.

climax.text: In 2022, the team released an image of Sagittarius A*—the black hole at the center of our own galaxy.

punchline.text: An Earth-sized telescope, petabytes of data, and the shadow of a monster revealed.

quiz.question: Why was the data from the Event Horizon Telescope shipped by airplane?
options: The data volume was so massive that internet transfer would have been too slow
The telescopes had no internet connection
Airplane transport was more secure against hacking
